nodes.label: P2.1 — the normalization x goodness grid (make-or-break gate)
nodes.description: 9-cell focused grid crossing {length-norm, layer-norm, group-norm, online-BN, batch-norm, no-norm} x {squared, linear} goodness, under threshold-free contrast loss, on CIFAR-10-flat, 5 seeds. Pass gate: any learning cell reaching depth-slope >= 0.

nodes.label: P2.1's 9-cell grid
nodes.description: Cells: len+sq(wall)=-0.010, len+lin=-0.019, layer+lin(HERO)=-0.020, layer+sq=COLLAPSED(dead 1.00), group+lin=-0.017, online-BN+lin(HERO)=-0.021, batch-BN+lin=-0.026, batch-BN+sq(Trifecta)=COLLAPSED, none+lin(cheat)=near-chance fail. Every learning cell declines; the two slope>=0 cells are degenerate collapses.

nodes.label: P2.1 verdict: GATE FAILED — 0/7 learning cells reach slope >= 0
nodes.description: Transmission fix (linear goodness + per-sample norm) works at the mechanism level (dead-units 0.25->0.00, erank 19->46-50, L1 probe up) but produces NO depth-rising class separability. New finding: squared goodness + mean-zero norm = catastrophic total unit death; length-norm survives only because it keeps the mean. Contrast loss ~doubles deep-layer probe vs two-sided theta.

nodes.label: squared-goodness deactivation cascade
nodes.description: Sum-squared goodness has an h-factor in its gradient that deactivates/freezes quiet units, causing a dead-unit cascade that compounds with depth (dead 0.02->0.47 in P2.0). Linear goodness removes the h-factor, keeping quiet units alive (dead ~0.00 in P2.1).
nodes.rationale: The DeeperForward causal story, confirmed and then sharpened by the mean-zero-norm death finding.

nodes.label: squared goodness + mean-zero norm = catastrophic unit death
nodes.description: layer-norm/batch-norm (mean-zero) combined with squared goodness kills ~half the units via ReLU (mean-zero centering makes them negative) and squared goodness's h-factor kills the rest -> dead 1.00, erank 0. Length-norm survives the wall only because it does NOT remove the mean.
nodes.rationale: A new finding sharpening DeeperForward's deactivation thesis; any future mean-zero cell must pair with linear goodness.

nodes.label: Decision: STOP/rethink -> route to P2.2 (objective), P2.3/P2.4 deprioritized
nodes.description: Per README §5's STOP discipline: since transmission cannot bend the slope, the rethink routes to the objective axis (class-aware negatives), promoted ahead of collaboration (P2.3) and interface (P2.4) rungs which presuppose a deep SCFF stack worth refining.
nodes.rationale: Carries forward the threshold-free contrast loss and layer-norm/linear-goodness transmission baseline into P2.2 unchanged, so only the objective varies.
